37	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
48	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
50	    # generated tokens
51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
53	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]
54	
55	
56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
71	      self.pop_removed())
72	    
73	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
74	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
75	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
76	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
77	    """
78	
79	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
80	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
81	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
82	    added: list[AddedRequest]
83	
84	    def __init__(
85	        self,
86	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
87	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
88	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        self._removed = removed or []
91	        self.moved = moved or []
92	        self.added = added or []
93	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
94	
95	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
96	        """Sort removed request indices in
97	        descending order.
98	        
99	        Idempotent after first call in a
100	        given step, until reset.
101	        """
102	        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
103	            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
104	            self._is_removed_sorted = True
105	
106	    @property
107	    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
108	        """Removed request indices sorted in
109	        descending order"""
110	        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
111	        return self._removed
112	
113	    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
114	        """Register the removal of a request from
115	        the persistent batch.
116	
117	        Must not be called after the first time
118	        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
119	        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
120	        
121	        Args:
122	          index: request index
123	        """
124	        if self._is_removed_sorted:
125	            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
126	                               " self.removed has been read.")
127	        self._removed.append(index)
128	
129	    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
130	        return bool(self._removed)
131	
132	    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
133	        """Return lowest removed request index"""
134	        if self.has_removed():
135	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
136	            return self._removed[-1]
137	        return None
138	
139	    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
140	        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
141	        if self.has_removed():
142	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
143	            return self._removed.pop()
144	        return None
145	
146	    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
147	        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
148	        and reset internal batch update builder state.
149	        
150	        Args:
151	          batch_size: current persistent batch size
152	
153	        Returns:
154	          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
155	        """
156	        # Reset removal-sorting logic
157	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
158	        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
159	            # No update; short-circuit
160	            return None
161	        # Build batch state update
162	        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
163	            batch_size=batch_size,
164	            removed=self._removed,
165	            moved=self.moved,
166	            added=self.added,
167	        )
168	        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
169	        self._removed = []
170	        self.moved = []
171	        self.added = []
172	        return batch_update
173

175	class LogitsProcessor(ABC):
176	
177	    @abstractmethod
178	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
179	        raise NotImplementedError
180	
181	    @abstractmethod
182	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
183	        """True if logits processor has no impact on the
184	        argmax computation in greedy sampling.
185	        NOTE: may or may not have the same value for all
186	        instances of a given LogitsProcessor subclass,
187	        depending on subclass implementation.
188	        TODO(andy): won't be utilized until logits
189	        processors are user-extensible
190	        """
191	        raise NotImplementedError
192	
193	    @abstractmethod
194	    def update_state(
195	        self,
196	        batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate],
197	    ) -> None:
198	        """Called when there are new output tokens, prior
199	        to each forward pass.
200	
201	        Args:
202	            batch_update is non-None iff there have been
203	            changes to the batch makeup.
204	        """
205	        raise NotImplementedError
206

208	@dataclass
209	class LogitsProcessorManager:
210	    """Encapsulates initialized logitsproc objects."""
211	    argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
212	        default_factory=list)  # argmax-invariant logitsprocs
213	    non_argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
214	        default_factory=list)  # non-argmax-invariant logitsprocs
215	
216	    @property
217	    def all(self) -> Iterator[LogitsProcessor]:
218	        """Iterator over all logits processors."""
219	        return chain(self.argmax_invariant, self.non_argmax_invariant)

224	
225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits
307	
308	
309	class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
310	
311	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
312	        super().__init__()
313	        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
314	        self.device = device
315	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
316	
317	        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
318	        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
319	                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))
320	
321	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
322	        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
323	        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
324	        return False
325	
326	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
327	        if not batch_update:
328	            return
329	
330	        # Process added requests.
331	        needs_update = bool(batch_update.added)
332	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
333	            if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) and (lb :=
334	                                                       params.logit_bias):
335	                self.biases[index] = lb
336	            else:
337	                self.biases.pop(index, None)
338	
339	        if self.biases:
340	            # Process removed requests.
341	            for index in batch_update.removed:
342	                if self.biases.pop(index, None):
343	                    needs_update = True
344	
345	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
346	            for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
347	                if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
348	                    if (a_entry := self.biases.pop(a_index, None)) is None:
349	                        if self.biases.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
350	                            needs_update = True
351	                    else:
352	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
353	                        needs_update = True
354	                else:
355	                    a_entry = self.biases.pop(a_index, None)
356	                    if (b_entry := self.biases.pop(b_index, None)) is not None:
357	                        self.biases[a_index] = b_entry
358	                        needs_update = True
359	                    if a_entry is not None:
360	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
361	                        needs_update = True
362	
363	        # Update tensors if needed.
364	        if needs_update:
365	            reqs, tok_ids, biases = [], [], []
366	            for req, lb in self.biases.items():
367	                reqs.extend([req] * len(lb))
368	                tok_ids.extend(lb.keys())
369	                biases.extend(lb.values())
370	
371	            self.bias_tensor = self._device_tensor(biases, torch.float32)
372	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
373	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
374	
375	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
376	        return (torch.tensor(data,
377	                             device="cpu",
378	                             dtype=dtype,
379	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
380	                                                            non_blocking=True))
381	
382	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
383	        if self.biases:
384	            logits[self.logits_slice] += self.bias_tensor
385	        return logits
386	
387	
388	class MinTokensLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
389	
390	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
391	        # index -> (min_toks, output_token_ids, stop_token_ids)
392	        super().__init__()
393	        self.min_toks: dict[int, tuple[int, Sequence[int], set[int]]] = {}
394	        self.device = device
395	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
396	
397	        # (req_idx_tensor,eos_tok_id_tensor)
398	        self.logits_slice: tuple[torch.Tensor,
399	                                 torch.Tensor] = (self._device_tensor(
400	                                     [], torch.int32),
401	                                                  self._device_tensor(
402	                                                      [], torch.int32))
403	
404	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
405	        """By censoring stop tokens, min-tokens can change the outcome
406	        of the argmax operation in greedy sampling."""
407	        return False
408	
409	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
410	        needs_update = False
411	
412	        if batch_update:
413	            # Process added requests.
414	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.added)
415	            for index, params, output_tok_ids in batch_update.added:
416	                if (isinstance(params, SamplingParams)
417	                        and (min_tokens := params.min_tokens)
418	                        and len(output_tok_ids) < min_tokens):
419	                    # Replace request metadata at batch index
420	                    self.min_toks[index] = (min_tokens, output_tok_ids,
421	                                            params.all_stop_token_ids)
422	                else:
423	                    # Drop request metadata at batch index
424	                    self.min_toks.pop(index, None)
425	
426	            if self.min_toks:
427	                # Process removed requests.
428	                for index in batch_update.removed:
429	                    if self.min_toks.pop(index, None):
430	                        needs_update = True
431	
432	                # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and
433	                # swapped (a<->b)
434	                for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
435	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
436	                        if (a_entry := self.min_toks.pop(a_index,
437	                                                         None)) is None:
438	                            if self.min_toks.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
439	                                needs_update = True
440	                        else:
441	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
442	                            needs_update = True
443	                    else:
444	                        a_entry = self.min_toks.pop(a_index, None)
445	                        if (b_entry := self.min_toks.pop(b_index,
446	                                                         None)) is not None:
447	                            self.min_toks[a_index] = b_entry
448	                            needs_update = True
449	                        if a_entry is not None:
450	                            self.min_toks[b_index] = a_entry
451	                            needs_update = True
452	
453	        if self.min_toks:
454	            # Check for any requests that have attained their min tokens.
455	            to_remove = tuple(index for index, (min_toks, out_tok_ids,
456	                                                _) in self.min_toks.items()
457	                              if len(out_tok_ids) >= min_toks)
458	            if to_remove:
459	                needs_update = True
460	                for index in to_remove:
461	                    del self.min_toks[index]
462	
463	        # Update tensors if needed.
464	        if needs_update:
465	            reqs: list[int] = []
466	            tok_ids: list[int] = []
467	            for req, (_, _, stop_tok_ids) in self.min_toks.items():
468	                reqs.extend([req] * len(stop_tok_ids))
469	                tok_ids.extend(stop_tok_ids)
470	
471	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
472	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
473	
474	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
475	        return (torch.tensor(data,
476	                             device="cpu",
477	                             dtype=dtype,
478	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
479	                                                            non_blocking=True))
480	
481	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
482	        if self.min_toks:
483	            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
484	            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
485	        return logits
486	
487	
488	def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
489	                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
490	    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
491	    loads by default.
492	
493	    Args:
494	      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
495	                            for use by logitsproc
496	      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
497	      device: inference device
498	
499	    Returns:
500	      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
501	    """
502	    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
503	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
504	    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
505	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
506	    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
507	        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
508	        device=device,
509	        # +1 for temporary swap space
510	        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
511	    return LogitsProcessorManager(
512	        non_argmax_invariant=[
513	            min_tokens_logitproc,
514	            logit_bias_logitproc,
515	        ],
516	        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
517	    )

3	from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams
4	from vllm.triton_utils import tl, triton
5	
6	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
7	
8	
9	def is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params: SamplingParams) -> bool:
10	    """True if request is incompatible with speculative decoding"""
11	    return (sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0
12	            or sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0
13	            or sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0
14	            or sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS
15	            or sampling_params.logprobs is not None)
16	
17	
18	@triton.jit
19	def prepare_eagle_input_kernel(
20	    out_ptr,
21	    cu_query_lens_ptr,
22	    cu_num_tokens_ptr,
23	    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,
24	):
25	    pid = tl.program_id(0)
26	
27	    # [start_pos, end_pos)
28	    start_pos = tl.load(cu_num_tokens_ptr + pid)
29	    end_pos = tl.load(cu_num_tokens_ptr + pid + 1)
30	    num_tokens = end_pos - start_pos
31	
32	    index_start = tl.load(cu_query_lens_ptr + pid)
33	
34	    num_blocks = tl.cdiv(num_tokens, BLOCK_SIZE)
35	    for i in tl.range(num_blocks):
36	        offset = i * BLOCK_SIZE + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)
37	        tl.store(
38	            out_ptr + start_pos + offset,
39	            index_start + offset,
40	            mask=offset < num_tokens,
41	        )

86	class SamplerOutput(
87	        msgspec.Struct,
88	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
89	        array_like=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
90	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
91	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
92	
93	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
94	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
95	    """
96	
97	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]
98	
99	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.
100	    sampled_token_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
101	
102	    # On-device tensor containing the logprobs of each token.
103	    logprobs: Optional["torch.Tensor"] = None
104	
105	    # Holds either (1) the pythonized sampler result (single-step scheduling)
106	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the
107	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)
108	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.
111	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
114	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
116	
117	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
118	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are
119	    # specified in lieu of prompt token ids or text.
120	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
121	
122	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.
123	    spec_decode_worker_metrics: Optional[SpecDecodeWorkerMetrics] = None
124	
125	    # Optional last hidden states from the model.
126	    hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
127	
128	    # Optional prefill hidden states from the model
129	    # (used for models like EAGLE).
130	    prefill_hidden_states: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
131	
132	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers
133	    model_forward_time: Optional[float] = None
134	
135	    # Time taken in the model execute function. This will include model forward,
136	    # block/sync across workers, cpu-gpu sync time and sampling time.
137	    model_execute_time: Optional[float] = None
138	
139	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
140	        return self.outputs[idx]
141	
142	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):
143	        self.outputs[idx] = value
144	
145	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]:
146	        return iter(self.outputs)
147	
148	    def __len__(self):
149	        return len(self.outputs)
150	
151	    def __eq__(self, other: object):
152	        return isinstance(other,
153	                          self.__class__) and self.outputs == other.outputs
154	
155	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
156	        """Show the shape of a tensor instead of its values to reduce noise.
157	        """
158	        sampled_token_probs_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_probs is None
159	                                    else self.sampled_token_probs.shape)
160	        sampled_token_ids_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_ids is None else
161	                                  self.sampled_token_ids.shape)
162	        return (
163	            f"SamplerOutput(outputs={self.outputs}, "
164	            f"sampled_token_probs={sampled_token_probs_repr}, "
165	            f"sampled_token_ids={sampled_token_ids_repr}, "
166	            f"spec_decode_worker_metrics={self.spec_decode_worker_metrics})")

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270

196	    def add_request(
197	        self,
198	        request: "CachedRequestState",
199	        req_index: Optional[int] = None,
200	    ) -> None:
201	        if req_index is None:
202	            req_index = self.num_reqs
203	        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
204	
205	        req_id = request.req_id
206	        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
207	            self._req_ids.append(req_id)
208	            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
209	        else:
210	            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
211	            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids
212	
213	        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index
214	
215	        # Copy the prompt token ids and output token ids.
216	        num_prompt_tokens = len(request.prompt_token_ids)
217	        self.num_prompt_tokens[req_index] = num_prompt_tokens
218	        self.token_ids_cpu[
219	            req_index, :num_prompt_tokens] = request.prompt_token_ids
220	        start_idx = num_prompt_tokens
221	        end_idx = start_idx + len(request.output_token_ids)
222	        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,
223	                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
224	        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
225	        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
226	        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
227	        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
228	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens
229	
230	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
231	        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)
232	
233	        sampling_params = request.sampling_params
234	        assert sampling_params is not None, "pooling requests not supported yet"
235	        if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
236	            # Avoid later division by zero.
237	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
238	            self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
239	        else:
240	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
241	            self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
242	
243	        self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
244	        if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
245	            self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
246	        top_k = sampling_params.top_k
247	        if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
248	            self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
249	        else:
250	            top_k = self.vocab_size
251	        self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
252	        self.min_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.min_p
253	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
254	            req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
255	        if sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
256	            self.min_p_reqs.add(req_id)
257	        if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
258	            self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
259	        self.presence_penalties_cpu[
260	            req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
261	        if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
262	            self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
263	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
264	            req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
265	        if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
266	            self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
267	        if sampling_params.min_tokens:
268	            self.min_tokens[req_index] = (sampling_params.min_tokens,
269	                                          sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids)
270	
271	        # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
272	        # do not have their own generator.
273	        if request.generator is not None:
274	            self.generators[req_index] = request.generator
275	
276	        if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
277	            self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
278	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
279	            self.num_prompt_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs

3	"""Sampler layer implementing TPU supported operations."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
9	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
10	from vllm.v1.sample.tpu.metadata import TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata
11	
12	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
13	
14	
15	class Sampler(nn.Module):
16	
17	    def __init__(self):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
20	
21	    def forward(
22	        self,
23	        logits: torch.Tensor,
24	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
25	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
26	        # Use float32 for the logits.
27	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
28	        # Sample the next token.
29	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
30	
31	        # These are TPU tensors.
32	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
33	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
34	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
35	            # token per request.
36	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
37	            logprobs_tensors=None)
38	        return sampler_output
39	
40	    def apply_temperature(
41	        self,
42	        logits: torch.Tensor,
43	        temp: torch.Tensor,
44	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
45	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
46	
47	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
48	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
49	
50	    def sample(
51	        self,
52	        logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
54	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
56	
57	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
58	
59	        # Apply temperature.
60	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
61	
62	        # Apply min_p.
63	        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
64	            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
65	
66	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
67	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
68	            logits,
69	            sampling_metadata.generators,
70	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
71	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
72	        )
73	
74	        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
75	                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
76	        return sampled
77	
78	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
79	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
80

3	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	import torch
7	
8	from vllm.v1.worker.tpu_input_batch import InputBatch
9	
10	DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
11	    temperature=-1.0,
12	    min_p=0.0,
13	    # strictly disabled for now
14	    top_k=0,
15	    top_p=1.0,
16	    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
17	    # presence_penalties=0.0,
18	    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
19	)
20	
21	
22	@dataclass
23	class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
24	    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
25	    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
26	    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
27	    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
28	
29	    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
30	    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
31	    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
32	
33	    all_greedy: bool = True
34	
35	    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
36	    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
37	    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
38	    logprobs: bool = False
39	
40	    # TODO No penalties for now
41	    no_penalties: bool = True
42	    prompt_token_ids = None
43	    frequency_penalties = None
44	    presence_penalties = None
45	    repetition_penalties = None
46	    # should use tensor
47	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())
48	
49	    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized
50	
51	    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
52	        default_factory=lambda: list())
53	
54	    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
55	    bad_words_token_ids = None
56	
57	    # Generator not supported by xla
58	    _generators: dict[int,
59	                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())
60	
61	    @property
62	    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
63	        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
64	        return self._generators
65	
66	    @classmethod
67	    def from_input_batch(
68	        cls,
69	        input_batch: InputBatch,
70	        padded_num_reqs: int,
71	        xla_device: torch.device,
72	        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
73	    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
74	        """
75	        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.
76	
77	        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
78	        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
79	        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.
80	
81	        Args:
82	            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
83	            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
84	            xla_device: The XLA device.
85	            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
86	                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
87	                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
88	                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
89	        """
90	        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
91	            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
92	        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
93	        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
94	                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
95	            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)
96	
97	        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs
98	
99	        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
100	            # Pad value is the default one.
101	            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val
102	
103	        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
104	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
105	        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
106	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
107	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
108	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
109	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
110	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
111	
112	        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
113	        return cls(
114	            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
115	            to(xla_device),
116	            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
117	            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
118	            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
119	                xla_device),

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the entire codebase. Let me compile the complete implementation plan.

Here is the detailed, step-by-step implementation plan for adding Top-A sampling to vLLM.

## Implementation Plan: Top-A Sampling in vLLM

### Overview

Top-A keeps only tokens whose probability `>= a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. When `top_a == 0.0`, it is disabled (no-op). This is functionally identical to the `min_p` algorithm -- both compute `threshold = parameter * max_prob` and mask tokens below that threshold. The implementation follows the exact same pattern as `min_p` across all code paths.

### Files to Modify (in dependency order)

---

### Step 1: `vllm/sampling_params.py` -- Add the `top_a` parameter to `SamplingParams`

This is the foundation; every other file depends on `SamplingParams.top_a` existing.

**1a. Add field declaration (after line 214)**

After `min_p: float = 0.0` (line 214), add:
```python
top_a: float = 0.0
```

**1b. Add docstring entry (after line 156)**

After the `min_p` docstring block (lines 154-156), add documentation for `top_a`:
```
top_a: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
    considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
    Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this. Also known as
    Top-A sampling.
```

**1c. Update `from_optional` static method**

- Add parameter at line ~261 (after `min_p: float = 0.0`): `top_a: float = 0.0`
- Add to the `SamplingParams()` constructor call at line ~304 (after `min_p=min_p`): `top_a=top_a,`

**1d. Update `__post_init__` greedy reset (line 381)**

After `self.min_p = 0.0` (line 381), add:
```python

```

**1e. Add validation in `_verify_args` (after line 428)**

After the `min_p` validation block (lines 426-428), add:

```python
if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.top_a}.")
```

**1f. Update `__repr__` (after line 572)**

After `f"min_p={self.min_p}, "` (line 572), add:


---

### Step 2: V0 Path -- `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

After `min_ps: torch.Tensor` (line 379), add:

### Step 3: V0 Path -- `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**3a. Add `_apply_top_a` function (after line 441, after `_apply_min_p`)**

This is identical to `_apply_min_p` but uses `top_a` tensor:
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**3b. Update `_init_sampling_tensors` (lines 216-223)**

Change the unpacking to include `do_top_a`:

```python
     sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
```

**3c. Update `forward` method (lines 265-289)**


```python
    logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
```

---

### Step 4: V1 Path -- `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

**4a. Create `TopALogitsProcessor` class (after line 306, after `MinPLogitsProcessor`)**

Model it exactly on `MinPLogitsProcessor` (lines 225-306), replacing every occurrence of `min_p` with `top_a`:

```python
class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0

        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Top-a never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def get_top_a_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.top_a_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            top_a = params.top_a if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.top_a_cpu[index] != top_a:
                needs_update = True
                self.top_a_cpu[index] = top_a
            if top_a:
                self.top_a_count += 1

        if self.top_a_count:
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.top_a_cpu[index]:
                    self.top_a_count -= 1

            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (top_a_a :=
                          self.top_a_cpu[adx]) != (top_a_b :=
                                                   self.top_a_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.top_a_cpu[bdx] = top_a_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.top_a_cpu[adx] = top_a_b

        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.top_a_count and (needs_update or self.top_a.shape[0] != size):
            self.top_a = self.top_a_device[:size]
            self.top_a.copy_(self.top_a_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.top_a.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits

        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

**4b. Register in `init_builtin_logitsprocs` (after line 510, line 516)**

After creating `min_p_logitproc`, create:

```python
top_a_logitproc = TopALogitsProcessor(
    pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
    device=device,
    max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
```

Add `top_a_logitproc` to the `argmax_invariant` list at line 516:
```python
argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc, top_a_logitproc],
```

### Step 5: V1 Spec Decode -- `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py`

**Line 14:** Add `or sampling_params.top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS` to the condition:

```python
def is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params: SamplingParams) -> bool:
    return (sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0
            or sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0
            or sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0
            or sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS
            or sampling_params.top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS
            or sampling_params.logprobs is not None)
```

---

### Step 6: V1 TPU Path -- Three files

The TPU path does NOT use the LogitsProcessor infrastructure; it handles sampling parameters directly in its own sampler. Therefore, `top_a` must also be added here.

**6a. `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`**

- After `self.min_p` / `self.min_p_cpu_tensor` / `self.min_p_cpu` / `self.min_p_reqs` declarations (lines 107-115), add identical declarations for `top_a`:
  ```python

  self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32, device="cpu", pin_memory=pin_memory)
  self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
  self.top_a_reqs: set[str] = set()
  ```

- In `add_request` (after line 256): Add `self.top_a_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_a` and `top_a_reqs.add(req_id)` logic.

- In `remove_request` (after line 332): Add `self.top_a_reqs.discard(req_id)`.

- In `swap` (after line 390): Add `self.top_a_cpu[i1], self.top_a_cpu[i2] = self.top_a_cpu[i2], self.top_a_cpu[i1]`.

- In `condense` (after line 474): Add `self.top_a_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_a_cpu[last_req_index]`.

- Add property (after line 567): `@property` / `def no_top_a(self) -> bool:` / `return len(self.top_a_reqs) == 0`.

**6b. `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`**

---

### Step 8: Benchmark CLI -- `vllm/benchmarks/serve.py`

### Step 9: Config -- `vllm/config.py`

---

### Step 10: Tests

**10a. `tests/v1/sample/test_logits_processors.py`:**

- Import `TopALogitsProcessor` (line 24, add to the import list).
- Add `_top_a_params` function (similar to `_min_p_params`) that sets `kwargs["top_a"]` to a random value.
- Add `_top_a_validate` function (similar to `_min_p_validate`).
- Add `TopALogitsProcessor` entry to `logitsprocs_test_mapping` dict (after line 407).
- Update `_sampling_params_from_logitproc` default kwargs (line 136) to include `"top_a": 0.0`.

**10b. `tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py`:**

- Add test cases for `top_a` parameter (following existing `min_p` test patterns).

---

### Implementation Order Summary

1. `vllm/sampling_params.py` -- The parameter definition (everything else depends on this)
2. `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` -- V0 tensor infrastructure
3. `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` -- V0 sampling logic
4. `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` -- V1 GPU logits processor
5. `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py` -- V1 spec decode compatibility
6. `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py` -- V1 TPU input batch
7. `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` -- V1 TPU sampling metadata
8. `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` -- V1 TPU sampler
9. `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` -- OpenAI API exposure
10. `vllm/benchmarks/serve.py` -- Benchmark CLI
11. `vllm/config.py` -- Generation config override support
12. Tests

### Key Architectural Decisions

1. **No combined min_p/top_a function**: Despite being algorithmically identical, `top_a` is implemented as a separate parameter and separate code path. This keeps the implementation simple and independently configurable. A user can set both `min_p=0.2` and `top_a=0.3`; both filters would apply sequentially (the more restrictive one wins).

2. **V1 GPU uses LogitsProcessor; V1 TPU does not**: The GPU V1 path uses the `LogitsProcessorManager` framework, so `TopALogitsProcessor` integrates cleanly. The TPU V1 path handles sampling parameters as direct tensor operations in its own sampler, so `top_a` must be threaded through `tpu_input_batch.py`, `tpu/metadata.py`, and `tpu/sampler.py` separately.

3. **Argmax-invariant**: `TopALogitsProcessor.is_argmax_invariant()` returns `True` because when `temperature=0` (greedy), `top_a` is reset to `0.0` in `__post_init__`, so it never affects greedy output. This means it goes in the `argmax_invariant` list and is only applied during random sampling.

4. **Spec decode incompatibility**: Like `min_p`, `top_a` is marked as incompatible with speculative decoding because probability-based filtering after temperature scaling can cause inconsistencies between draft and target models.

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397080318-kdx5dy/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397080318-kdx5dy/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397080318-kdx5dy/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397080318-kdx5dy/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

Design an implementation plan for adding "Top-A" sampling to vLLM. Top-A keeps only tokens whose probability >= `a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. When top_a == 0.0, it's disabled (no-op).

## Important context from codebase exploration

There are TWO separate code paths in vLLM: V0 (legacy) and V1 (new). Both need to be updated.

### Key insight: top_a is semantically identical to min_p
Looking at the existing `min_p` implementation, **Top-A is functionally the same algorithm as min_p**: both filter tokens where `p(token) < threshold * max_p`. The ONLY difference is the parameter name. However, the user wants a separate `top_a` parameter, so we implement it as a separate knob that follows the exact same pattern as `min_p`.

### Files to modify and what to do:

**1. `vllm/sampling_params.py` (SamplingParams class)**
- Line 214: `min_p: float = 0.0` → add `top_a: float = 0.0` after it (line ~215)
- Lines 150-156: Add docstring entry for `top_a` after `min_p` docs
- Line 381: In `__post_init__`, when temperature < epsilon (greedy), reset `top_a = 0.0` alongside `min_p = 0.0`
- Line 426-428: In `_verify_args()`, add validation: `if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0: raise ValueError(...)`

**2. `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (V0 sampler - direct tensor ops)**
- The `_apply_min_p` function (lines 427-441) does EXACTLY the top-a algorithm: softmax → find max prob → mask where prob < threshold * max_prob → fill -inf. We need a `_apply_top_a` function that does the same thing, or combine them.
- In `Sampler.forward()` (lines 284-295): After `do_min_p` check at line 288, add a `do_top_a` check and call `_apply_top_a`.
- The `_init_sampling_tensors` method (line 216) needs to receive `do_top_a` flag.

**3. `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (V0 SamplingTensors)**
- `SamplingTensors` dataclass (line 372-384): Add `top_as: torch.Tensor` field
- `from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 386-495):
  - Add `top_as: list[float] = []` (line ~399)
  - Add `do_top_a = False` flag (line ~405)
  - Extract `top_a = sampling_params.top_a` (line ~416)
  - Add detection: `if not do_top_a and top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS: do_top_a = True` (line ~430)
  - Replicate `top_a` into the lists for prefill and sample phases (lines ~446, ~457)
  - Pass `top_as` to `from_lists()` (line ~481)
  - Return `do_top_a` as a 4th boolean (currently returns 3: do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p) → change to 4 booleans
- `from_lists()` (lines 498-597): Accept `top_as: list[float]`, create tensor, transfer to device
- Return signature changes from `tuple[SamplingTensors, bool, bool, bool]` to `tuple[SamplingTensors, bool, bool, bool, bool]`

**4. `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` (V1 logits processor)**
- Create a new `TopALogitsProcessor` class modeled EXACTLY on `MinPLogitsProcessor` (lines 225-306). It's the same algorithm with different parameter names: `top_a` instead of `min_p`.
- Register it in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()` (lines 490-517): instantiate and add to `argmax_invariant` list since top-a never affects greedy sampling.

**5. `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py` (V1 spec decode compatibility)**
- In `is_spec_decode_unsupported()` (line 14): Add `or sampling_params.top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS` since top_a (like min_p) is incompatible with spec decode.

Here is the detailed, step-by-step implementation plan for adding Top-A sampling to vLLM.

## Implementation Plan: Top-A Sampling in vLLM

### Overview

Top-A keeps only tokens whose probability `>= a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. When `top_a == 0.0`, it is disabled (no-op). This is functionally identical to the `min_p` algorithm -- both compute `threshold = parameter * max_prob` and mask tokens below that threshold. The implementation follows the exact same pattern as `min_p` across all code paths.

### Files to Modify (in dependency order)

---

### Step 1: `vllm/sampling_params.py` -- Add the `top_a` parameter to `SamplingParams`

This is the foundation; every other file depends on `SamplingParams.top_a` existing.

**1a. Add field declaration (after line 214)**

After `min_p: float = 0.0` (line 214), add:


**1b. Add docstring entry (after line 156)**

After the `min_p` docstring block (lines 154-156), add documentation for `top_a`:
```
top_a: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
    considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
    Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this. Also known as
    Top-A sampling.
```

**1c. Update `from_optional` static method**

- Add parameter at line ~261 (after `min_p: float = 0.0`): `top_a: float = 0.0`
- Add to the `SamplingParams()` constructor call at line ~304 (after `min_p=min_p`): `top_a=top_a,`

**1d. Update `__post_init__` greedy reset (line 381)**

After `self.min_p = 0.0` (line 381), add:


**1e. Add validation in `_verify_args` (after line 428)**

After the `min_p` validation block (lines 426-428), add:

```python
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "

```

**1f. Update `__repr__` (after line 572)**

After `f"min_p={self.min_p}, "` (line 572), add:


### Step 3: V0 Path -- `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**3a. Add `_apply_top_a` function (after line 441, after `_apply_min_p`)**

This is identical to `_apply_min_p` but uses `top_a` tensor:
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

### Step 4: V1 Path -- `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`

**4a. Create `TopALogitsProcessor` class (after line 306, after `MinPLogitsProcessor`)**

Model it exactly on `MinPLogitsProcessor` (lines 225-306), replacing every occurrence of `min_p` with `top_a`:

```python
class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0

        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Top-a never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def get_top_a_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.top_a_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            top_a = params.top_a if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.top_a_cpu[index] != top_a:
                needs_update = True
                self.top_a_cpu[index] = top_a
            if top_a:
                self.top_a_count += 1

        if self.top_a_count:
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.top_a_cpu[index]:
                    self.top_a_count -= 1

            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (top_a_a :=
                          self.top_a_cpu[adx]) != (top_a_b :=
                                                   self.top_a_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.top_a_cpu[bdx] = top_a_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.top_a_cpu[adx] = top_a_b

        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.top_a_count and (needs_update or self.top_a.shape[0] != size):
            self.top_a = self.top_a_device[:size]
            self.top_a.copy_(self.top_a_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.top_a.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits

        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

**4b. Register in `init_builtin_logitsprocs` (after line 510, line 516)**

```python
    pin_memory=pin_memory_available,

```

```python
            or sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0
```

---

### Implementation Order Summary

1. `vllm/sampling_params.py` -- The parameter definition (everything else depends on this)
2. `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` -- V0 tensor infrastructure
3. `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` -- V0 sampling logic
4. `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` -- V1 GPU logits processor
5. `vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py` -- V1 spec decode compatibility
6. `vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py` -- V1 TPU input batch
7. `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` -- V1 TPU sampling metadata
8. `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` -- V1 TPU sampler
9. `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` -- OpenAI API exposure
10. `vllm/benchmarks/serve.py` -- Benchmark CLI
11. `vllm/config.py` -- Generation config override support
12. Tests

### Key Architectural Decisions

1. **No combined min_p/top_a function**: Despite being algorithmically identical, `top_a` is implemented as a separate parameter and separate code path. This keeps the implementation simple and independently configurable. A user can set both `min_p=0.2` and `top_a=0.3`; both filters would apply sequentially (the more restrictive one wins).

2. **V1 GPU uses LogitsProcessor; V1 TPU does not**: The GPU V1 path uses the `LogitsProcessorManager` framework, so `TopALogitsProcessor` integrates cleanly. The TPU V1 path handles sampling parameters as direct tensor operations in its own sampler, so `top_a` must be threaded through `tpu_input_batch.py`, `tpu/metadata.py`, and `tpu/sampler.py` separately.

3. **Argmax-invariant**: `TopALogitsProcessor.is_argmax_invariant()` returns `True` because when `temperature=0` (greedy), `top_a` is reset to `0.0` in `__post_init__`, so it never affects greedy output. This means it goes in the `argmax_invariant` list and is only applied during random sampling.

4. **Spec decode incompatibility**: Like `min_p`, `top_a` is marked as incompatible with speculative decoding because probability-based filtering after temperature scaling can cause inconsistencies between draft and target models.

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397080318-kdx5dy/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
        min_tokens: int = 0,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        detokenize: bool = True,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> "SamplingParams":
        if logit_bias is not None:
            # Convert token_id to integer
            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
            logit_bias = {
                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
            }

        return SamplingParams(
            n=1 if n is None else n,
            best_of=best_of,
            presence_penalty=0.0
            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
            frequency_penalty=0.0
            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
            repetition_penalty=1.0
            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            seed=seed,
            stop=stop,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            bad_words=bad_words,
            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            min_tokens=min_tokens,
            logprobs=logprobs,
            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
            detokenize=detokenize,
            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
            logits_processors=logits_processors,
            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
            output_kind=output_kind,
            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
            logit_bias=logit_bias,
            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
            extra_args=extra_args,
        )

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.worker.tpu_input_batch import InputBatch

DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
    temperature=-1.0,
    min_p=0.0,
    # strictly disabled for now
    top_k=0,
    top_p=1.0,
    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
    # presence_penalties=0.0,
    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
)


@dataclass
class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
    temperature: torch.Tensor = None

    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
    top_p: torch.Tensor = None

    all_greedy: bool = True

    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
    logprobs: bool = False

    # TODO No penalties for now
    no_penalties: bool = True
    prompt_token_ids = None
    frequency_penalties = None
    presence_penalties = None
    repetition_penalties = None
    # should use tensor
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())

    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized

    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
        default_factory=lambda: list())

    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
    bad_words_token_ids = None

    # Generator not supported by xla
    _generators: dict[int,
                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())

    @property
    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
        return self._generators

    @classmethod
    def from_input_batch(
        cls,
        input_batch: InputBatch,
        padded_num_reqs: int,
        xla_device: torch.device,
        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
        """
        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.

        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.

        Args:
            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
            xla_device: The XLA device.
            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
        """
        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)

        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs

        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
            # Pad value is the default one.
            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val

        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])

        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
        return cls(
            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
            to(xla_device),
            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
                xla_device),
            logprobs=needs_logprobs)

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	"""Sampler layer implementing TPU supported operations."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
9	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
10	from vllm.v1.sample.tpu.metadata import TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata
11	
12	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
13	
14	
15	class Sampler(nn.Module):
16	
17	    def __init__(self):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
20	
21	    def forward(
22	        self,
23	        logits: torch.Tensor,
24	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
25	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
26	        # Use float32 for the logits.
27	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
28	        # Sample the next token.
29	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
30	
31	        # These are TPU tensors.
32	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
33	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
34	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
35	            # token per request.
36	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
37	            logprobs_tensors=None)
38	        return sampler_output
39	
40	    def apply_temperature(
41	        self,
42	        logits: torch.Tensor,
43	        temp: torch.Tensor,
44	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
45	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
46	
47	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
48	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
49	
50	    def sample(
51	        self,
52	        logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
54	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
56	
57	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
58	
59	        # Apply temperature.
60	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
61	
62	        # Apply min_p.
63	        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
64	            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
65	
66	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
67	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
68	            logits,
69	            sampling_metadata.generators,
70	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
71	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
72	        )
73	
74	        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
75	                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
76	        return sampled
77	
78	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
79	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
80	
81	    def gather_logprobs(
82	        self,
83	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
84	        num_logprobs: int,
85	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
86	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
87	        """
88	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
89	
90	        Args:
91	          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
92	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
93	                        retain per token
94	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
95	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
96	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
97	                     with (num tokens) elements
98	
99	        Returns:
100	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
101	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
102	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
103	        """
104	        # Find the topK values.
105	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
106	                                                 num_logprobs,
107	                                                 dim=-1)
108	
109	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
110	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
111	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
112	
113	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
114	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
115	
116	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
117	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122	
123	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
124	
125	    def apply_min_p(
126	        self,
127	        logits: torch.Tensor,
128	        min_p: torch.Tensor,
129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
130	        """
131	        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
144	        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
145	        return logits
146
