232	        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,
233	                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
234	        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
235	        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
236	        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
237	        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
238	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens
239	
240	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
241	        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)
242	
243	        sampling_params = request.sampling_params
244	        assert sampling_params is not None, "pooling requests not supported yet"
245	        if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
246	            # Avoid later division by zero.
247	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
248	            self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
249	        else:
250	            self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
251	            self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
252	
253	        self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
254	        if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
255	            self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
256	        top_k = sampling_params.top_k
257	        if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
258	            self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
259	        else:
260	            top_k = self.vocab_size
261	        self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
262	        self.min_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.min_p
263	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
264	            req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
265	        if sampling_params.min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
266	            self.min_p_reqs.add(req_id)
267	        self.top_a_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_a
268	        if sampling_params.top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS:
269	            self.top_a_reqs.add(req_id)
270	        if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
271	            self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
272	        self.presence_penalties_cpu[
273	            req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
274	        if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
275	            self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
276	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
277	            req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
278	        if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
279	            self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
280	        if sampling_params.min_tokens:
281	            self.min_tokens[req_index] = (sampling_params.min_tokens,
282	                                          sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids)
283	
284	        # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
285	        # do not have their own generator.
286	        if request.generator is not None:
287	            self.generators[req_index] = request.generator
288	
289	        if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
290	            self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
291	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
292	            self.num_prompt_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
293	        if sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
294	            self.logit_bias[req_index] = sampling_params.logit_bias
295	
296	        if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:
297	            self.has_allowed_token_ids.add(req_id)
298	            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is None:
299	                # Lazy allocation for this tensor, which can be large.
300	                # False means we don't fill with -inf.
301	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask = torch.zeros(self.max_num_reqs,
302	                                                          self.vocab_size,
303	                                                          dtype=torch.bool,
304	                                                          device=self.device)
305	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
306	                    self.max_num_reqs,
307	                    self.vocab_size,
308	                    dtype=torch.bool,
309	                    device="cpu")
310	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index] = True
311	            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
312	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index][
313	                sampling_params.allowed_token_ids] = False
314	
315	        if sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids:
316	            self.bad_words_token_ids[
317	                req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids
318	
319	        # Add request lora ID
320	        if request.lora_request:
321	            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id
322	            if lora_id not in self.lora_id_to_request_ids:
323	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id] = set()
324	
325	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = lora_id
326	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].add(request.req_id)
327	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request[lora_id] = request.lora_request
328	        else:
329	            # No LoRA
330	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
331	
332	    def remove_request(self, req_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
333	        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense()."""
334	
335	        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
336	        if req_index is None:
337	            return None
338	        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
339	        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None
340	
341	        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
342	        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
343	        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
344	        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
345	        self.min_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
346	        self.top_a_reqs.discard(req_id)
347	        self.min_tokens.pop(req_index, None)
348	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
349	        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
350	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
351	        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
352	        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
353	        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
354	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)
355	
356	        # LoRA
357	        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
358	        if lora_id != 0:
359	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
360	            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
361	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
362	                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
363	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
364	
365	        self.logit_bias[req_index] = None
366	        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
367	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
368	            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
369	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
370	        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
371	        return req_index
372	
373	    def swap_states(self, i1: int, i2: int) -> None:
374	        old_id_i1 = self._req_ids[i1]
375	        old_id_i2 = self._req_ids[i2]
376	        self._req_ids[i1], self._req_ids[i2] =\
377	            self._req_ids[i2], self._req_ids[i1] # noqa
378	        self.req_output_token_ids[i1], self.req_output_token_ids[i2] =\
379	            self.req_output_token_ids[i2], self.req_output_token_ids[i1]
380	        assert old_id_i1 is not None and old_id_i2 is not None
381	        self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2] =\
382	            self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1]
383	        self.num_tokens[i1], self.num_tokens[i2] =\
384	            self.num_tokens[i2], self.num_tokens[i1]
385	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2] =\
386	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1]
387	        self.num_prompt_tokens[i1], self.num_prompt_tokens[i2] =\
388	            self.num_prompt_tokens[i2], self.num_prompt_tokens[i1]
389	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2] =\
390	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1]
391	        self.temperature_cpu[i1], self.temperature_cpu[i2] =\
392	            self.temperature_cpu[i2], self.temperature_cpu[i1]
393	        self.top_p_cpu[i1], self.top_p_cpu[i2] =\
394	            self.top_p_cpu[i2], self.top_p_cpu[i1]
395	        self.top_k_cpu[i1], self.top_k_cpu[i2] =\
396	            self.top_k_cpu[i2], self.top_k_cpu[i1]
397	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
398	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1]
399	        self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
400	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1]
401	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
402	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1]
403	        self.min_p_cpu[i1], self.min_p_cpu[i2] =\
404	            self.min_p_cpu[i2], self.min_p_cpu[i1]
405	        self.top_a_cpu[i1], self.top_a_cpu[i2] = \
406	            self.top_a_cpu[i2], self.top_a_cpu[i1]
407	
408	        # NOTE: the following is unsafe
409	        # self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], =\
410	        #     self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...]
411	        # instead, we need to temporiarily copy the data for one of the indices
412	        # TODO(lucas): optimize this by only copying valid indices
413	        tmp = self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...].copy()
414	        self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...] = self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...]
415	        self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...] = tmp
416	
417	        swap_dict_values(self.generators, i1, i2)
418	        swap_dict_values(self.min_tokens, i1, i2)
419	        swap_dict_values(self.bad_words_token_ids, i1, i2)
420	
421	        self.request_lora_mapping[i1], self.request_lora_mapping[i2] =\
422	            self.request_lora_mapping[i2], self.request_lora_mapping[i1]
423	        self.logit_bias[i1], self.logit_bias[i2] =\
424	            self.logit_bias[i2], self.logit_bias[i1]
425	
426	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
427	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1], \
428	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2] =\
429	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2], \
430	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1]
431	        self.block_table.swap_row(i1, i2)
432	
433	    def condense(self, empty_req_indices: list[int]) -> None:
434	        """Move non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
435	        
436	        Args:
437	          empty_req_indices: empty batch indices, sorted descending.
438	        """
439	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
440	        if num_reqs == 0:
441	            # The batched states are empty.
442	            self._req_ids.clear()
443	            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
444	            return
445	
446	        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
447	        # is sorted in descending order.
448	        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
449	        while empty_req_indices:
450	            # Find the largest non-empty index.
451	            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
452	                last_req_index -= 1
453	
454	            # Find the smallest empty index.
455	            empty_index = empty_req_indices.pop()
456	            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
457	                break
458	
459	            # Swap the states.
460	            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
461	            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
462	            assert req_id is not None
463	            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
464	            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
465	            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
466	            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
467	            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index
468	
469	            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
470	            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
471	                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
472	            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
473	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
474	                last_req_index]
475	            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
476	                last_req_index]
477	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
478	                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
479	            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
480	            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
481	                last_req_index]
482	            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
483	            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
484	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
485	                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
486	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
487	                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
488	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
489	                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
490	            self.min_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.min_p_cpu[last_req_index]
491	            self.top_a_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_a_cpu[last_req_index]
492	            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
493	            if generator is not None:
494	                self.generators[empty_index] = generator
495	
496	            min_token = self.min_tokens.pop(last_req_index, None)
497	            if min_token is not None:
498	                self.min_tokens[empty_index] = min_token
499	
500	            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
501	                last_req_index]
502	
503	            self.logit_bias[empty_index] = self.logit_bias[last_req_index]
504	
505	            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
506	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
507	                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
508	                        last_req_index]
509	
510	            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
511	                last_req_index, None)
512	            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
513	                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids
514	            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
515	            last_req_index -= 1
516	
517	        # Trim lists to the batch size.
518	        del self._req_ids[self.num_reqs:]
519	        del self.req_output_token_ids[self.num_reqs:]
520	
521	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
522	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
523	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
524	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
525	            device="cpu",
526	            dtype=torch.int64,
527	            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
528	        )
529	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
530	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
531	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
532	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
533	        # token_id of this value.
534	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
535	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
536	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
537	                                              non_blocking=True)
538	
539	    def make_lora_inputs(
540	        self, num_scheduled_tokens: np.ndarray
541	    ) -> tuple[tuple[int, ...], tuple[int, ...], set[LoRARequest]]:
542	        """
543	        Given the num_scheduled_tokens for each request in the batch, return
544	        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
545	        Returns:
546	            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
547	               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
548	            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
549	               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.
550	            3. lora_requests: Set of relevant LoRA requests.
551	        """
552	
553	        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]
554	        prompt_lora_mapping = tuple(req_lora_mapping)
555	        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
556	            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
557	        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
558	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
559	
560	        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests
561	
562	    @property
563	    def num_reqs(self) -> int:
564	        return len(self.req_id_to_index)
565	
566	    @property
567	    def all_greedy(self) -> bool:
568	        return len(self.random_reqs) == 0
569	
570	    @property
571	    def all_random(self) -> bool:
572	        return len(self.greedy_reqs) == 0
573	
574	    @property
575	    def no_top_p(self) -> bool:
576	        return len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0
577	
578	    @property
579	    def no_top_k(self) -> bool:
580	        return len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0
581	
582	    @property
583	    def no_min_p(self) -> bool:
584	        return len(self.min_p_reqs) == 0
585	
586	    @property
587	    def no_top_a(self) -> bool:
588	        return len(self.top_a_reqs) == 0
589	
590	    @property
591	    def no_penalties(self) -> bool:
592	        return (len(self.presence_penalties_reqs) == 0
593	                and len(self.frequency_penalties_reqs) == 0
594	                and len(self.repetition_penalties_reqs) == 0)
595	
596	    @property
597	    def max_num_logprobs(self) -> Optional[int]:
598	        return max(self.num_logprobs.values()) if self.num_logprobs else None
599	
600	    @property
601	    def no_prompt_logprob(self) -> bool:
602	        return not self.num_prompt_logprobs
603	
604	    @property
605	    def no_allowed_token_ids(self) -> bool:
606	        return len(self.has_allowed_token_ids) == 0

3	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
4	from typing import Optional

6	import torch

8	from vllm.v1.worker.tpu_input_batch import InputBatch
9	
10	DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
11	    temperature=-1.0,
12	    min_p=0.0,
13	    top_a=0.0,
14	    # strictly disabled for now
15	    top_k=0,
16	    top_p=1.0,
17	    # frequency_penalties=0.0,
18	    # presence_penalties=0.0,
19	    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
20	)

22	
23	@dataclass
24	class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
25	    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
26	    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
27	    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
28	    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
29	
30	    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
31	    top_a: torch.Tensor = None
32	    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
33	    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
34	
35	    all_greedy: bool = True
36	
37	    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
38	    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
39	    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
40	    logprobs: bool = False
41	
42	    # TODO No penalties for now
43	    no_penalties: bool = True
44	    prompt_token_ids = None
45	    frequency_penalties = None
46	    presence_penalties = None
47	    repetition_penalties = None
48	    # should use tensor
49	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())
50	
51	    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized
52	
53	    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
54	        default_factory=lambda: list())
55	
56	    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
57	    bad_words_token_ids = None
58	
59	    # Generator not supported by xla
60	    _generators: dict[int,
61	                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())
62	
63	    @property
64	    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
65	        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
66	        return self._generators
67	
68	    @classmethod
69	    def from_input_batch(
70	        cls,
71	        input_batch: InputBatch,
72	        padded_num_reqs: int,
73	        xla_device: torch.device,
74	        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
75	    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
76	        """
77	        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.
78	
79	        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
80	        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
81	        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.
82	
83	        Args:
84	            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
85	            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
86	            xla_device: The XLA device.
87	            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
88	                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
89	                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
90	                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
91	        """
92	        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
93	            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
94	        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
95	        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
96	                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
97	            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)
98	
99	        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs
100	
101	        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
102	            # Pad value is the default one.

def is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params: SamplingParams) -> bool:

class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0

        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        # Pre-allocated device tensor
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        # Current slice of the device tensor
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Top-a never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def get_top_a_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.top_a_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        # Process added requests.
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            top_a = params.top_a if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.top_a_cpu[index] != top_a:
                needs_update = True
                self.top_a_cpu[index] = top_a
            if top_a:
                self.top_a_count += 1

        if self.top_a_count:
            # Process removed requests.
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.top_a_cpu[index]:
                    self.top_a_count -= 1

            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (top_a_a :=
                          self.top_a_cpu[adx]) != (top_a_b :=
                                                   self.top_a_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.top_a_cpu[bdx] = top_a_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.top_a_cpu[adx] = top_a_b

        # Update tensors if needed.
        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.top_a_count and (needs_update or self.top_a.shape[0] != size):
            self.top_a = self.top_a_device[:size]
            self.top_a.copy_(self.top_a_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.top_a.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits

        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Adjust top_a threshold
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

    def apply_top_a(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        top_a: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Filters logits using Top-A adaptive probability thresholding.
        """
        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Reshape top_a for broadcasting
        adjusted_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_top_a
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
        return logits
```

assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
        do_top_a = self._do_top_a

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        if do_top_a:
            logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

return logits


def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits

content.text:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of all changes implemented:

## Implementation Complete — Top-A Sampling in vLLM

### Files modified (11 total):

| # | File | Changes |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | **`vllm/sampling_params.py`** | Added `top_a: float = 0.0` field, docstring, `from_optional` param, greedy reset, `[0,1]` validation, `__repr__` |
| 2 | **`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`** | Added `top_as` tensor to `SamplingTensors`, threading through `from_sampling_metadata` and `from_lists`, added `do_top_a` flag |
| 3 | **`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`** | Added `_apply_top_a()` function (softmax→max→threshold→mask), wired into `forward()` with `do_top_a` flag |
| 4 | **`vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`** | Added `TopALogitsProcessor` class (mirrors `MinPLogitsProcessor`), registered in `init_builtin_logitsprocs` as argmax-invariant |
| 5 | **`vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py`** | Added `top_a > eps` check to `is_spec_decode_unsupported()` |
| 6 | **`vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`** | Added `top_a` tensors, tracking sets, and logic in `add_request`/`remove_request`/`swap`/`condense` + `no_top_a` property |
| 7 | **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`** | Added `top_a` to defaults, dataclass field, `fill_slice`, and `cls()` return |
| 8 | **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py`** | Added `apply_top_a()` method and call in `sample()` |
| 9 | **`vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`** | Added `top_a` field + default + extraction + passthrough in both `ChatCompletionRequest` and `CompletionRequest` |
| 10 | **`vllm/config.py`** | Added `"top_a"` to `available_params` for generation config overrides |
| 11 | **`vllm/benchmarks/serve.py`** | Added `--top-a` CLI argument and dict entry |

### Algorithm (all paths):
```
probs = softmax(logits)
max_prob = max(probs, dim=-1)
mask tokens where probs < top_a * max_prob  →  set to -inf
```
When `top_a == 0.0`, filtering is skipped (no-op) via boolean flags / count checks.

## Implementation Complete — Top-A Sampling in vLLM

### Files modified (11 total):

| # | File | Changes |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | **`vllm/sampling_params.py`** | Added `top_a: float = 0.0` field, docstring, `from_optional` param, greedy reset, `[0,1]` validation, `__repr__` |
| 2 | **`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`** | Added `top_as` tensor to `SamplingTensors`, threading through `from_sampling_metadata` and `from_lists`, added `do_top_a` flag |
| 3 | **`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`** | Added `_apply_top_a()` function (softmax→max→threshold→mask), wired into `forward()` with `do_top_a` flag |
| 4 | **`vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`** | Added `TopALogitsProcessor` class (mirrors `MinPLogitsProcessor`), registered in `init_builtin_logitsprocs` as argmax-invariant |
| 5 | **`vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py`** | Added `top_a > eps` check to `is_spec_decode_unsupported()` |
| 6 | **`vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`** | Added `top_a` tensors, tracking sets, and logic in `add_request`/`remove_request`/`swap`/`condense` + `no_top_a` property |
| 7 | **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`** | Added `top_a` to defaults, dataclass field, `fill_slice`, and `cls()` return |
| 8 | **`vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py`** | Added `apply_top_a()` method and call in `sample()` |
| 9 | **`vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`** | Added `top_a` field + default + extraction + passthrough in both `ChatCompletionRequest` and `CompletionRequest` |
| 10 | **`vllm/config.py`** | Added `"top_a"` to `available_params` for generation config overrides |
| 11 | **`vllm/benchmarks/serve.py`** | Added `--top-a` CLI argument and dict entry |

### Algorithm (all paths):
```
probs = softmax(logits)
max_prob = max(probs, dim=-1)
mask tokens where probs < top_a * max_prob  →  set to -inf
```
When `top_a == 0.0`, filtering is skipped (no-op) via boolean flags / count checks.
